obsidian-ember.$description: Obsidian Ember — Primary dark canvas — page background in dark sections, card surface, deepest borders. The black-with-warmth that defines the brand atmosphere

dark-cocoa.$description: Dark Cocoa — Secondary dark surface, deep link borders, icon outlines — one step lifted from obsidian for layering depth

molten-core.$description: Molten Core — Inset glow shadow source, deep red-brown accents — the pressed-ember tone that gives buttons their internal fire

signal-red.$description: Signal Red — Outlined/ghost action border, nav borders, icon strokes, interactive outlines — the chromatic action color used for outlined buttons and active control edges

flare-orange.$description: Flare Orange — Primary brand accent — links, decorative borders, the hero gradient spectrum, highlight washes. The single hottest color in the system; used sparingly as functional punctuation

warm-blush.$description: Warm Blush — Soft surface wash, secondary peach background, decorative highlight fields — the diffused ember tone for breathing sections

vapor-peach.$description: Vapor Peach — Light section background, light-mode card surface, body text on dark, ghost button text — the warm off-white that breaks the dark rhythm

linen.$description: Linen — Primary text on dark, crisp borders on dark sections, inverse text. The near-white that carries most typography on dark surfaces

warm-ash.$description: Warm Ash — Muted button borders on light sections, tertiary text, subtle dividers — a warm gray that softens edges without going cold

pure-black.$description: Pure Black — Decorative SVG fills, icon line art, graphical accents — high-contrast details that read as deep-ink on the warm canvas

mist.$description: Mist — Input field borders on light backgrounds — the only cool-neutral in the system, used minimally for form controls

matter.$description: Primary typeface — body, display, headings, buttons, navigation, icons. Weight 700 carries display headlines; 500 for emphasis in body; 400 for regular text. Tight tracking across all sizes creates a compressed, confident read.

fabrikatmono.$description: Monospace accent — section labels, tags, technical metadata, trust signals. Used in uppercase for eyebrow labels like 'THE PROBLEM' and 'SOLUTIONS'. The technical-utility voice that contrasts Matter's editorial confidence.

vapor-peach.$description: Surface level 1: Light section background — problem statement, alternating breathing space

obsidian-ember.$description: Surface level 2: Primary dark canvas — hero, investor logos, solutions, footer

dark-cocoa.$description: Surface level 3: Elevated card surface on dark — slightly lifted from canvas for card depth

molten-core.$description: Surface level 4: Accent surface — button fills with inset glow, emphasis panels
